data.title: Bitcoin's Role Reassessed Amid Cooling Inflation Data
data.description:
Bitcoin investors are reevaluating their reasons for holding the cryptocurrency as inflation data shows signs of cooling. According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin entrepreneur Anthony Pompliano discussed this shift during an interview with Fox Business, questioning whether investors can maintain their belief in Bitcoin's value proposition without the immediate pressure of high inflation. Pompliano emphasized Bitcoin's finite supply as a key factor, suggesting that if money printing continues, Bitcoin's value could rise. He also highlighted Bitcoin and gold as strong long-term investments. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) dropped to 2.4% in January from 2.7% in December, as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. However, Mark Zandi, Moody’s chief economist, expressed skepticism, noting that inflation appears more favorable on paper than in reality.

Bitcoin is often viewed as a hedge against inflation due to its limited supply of 21 million coins. When central banks increase the money supply, leading to a decline in fiat currency value, investors frequently turn to assets like Bitcoin to safeguard their purchasing power. Despite this, sentiment around Bitcoin has reached multi-year lows, with the Crypto Fear & Greed Index indicating an "Extreme Fear" score of 9, a level not seen since June 2022. Over the past 30 days, Bitcoin has experienced a 28.14% decline, trading at $68,850 at the time of publication, according to CoinMarketCap.

Pompliano also addressed the macroeconomic environment, suggesting it could cause short-term volatility for Bitcoin before it resumes its upward trend. He predicted deflationary forces in the short term, leading to calls for money printing and interest rate reductions. This, he argued, would result in the devaluation of the US dollar, though the impact might not be immediately apparent. Pompliano referred to this phenomenon as a "monetary slingshot," where deflation masks the currency's devaluation. He anticipated that the Federal Reserve would continue expanding the money supply to manage inflation, ultimately making Bitcoin "more valuable than ever" as the dollar weakens. The US dollar index, which measures the dollar's strength against a basket of major currencies, has decreased by 2.32% over the past 30 days, trading at $96.88, according to TradingView.

data.title: AI and Anthropic's Market Prospects Discussed
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X about the positive outlook for AI and Anthropic. The investor expressed confidence in the companies' products, suggesting that they are self-sustaining in the market. However, the investor preferred not to delve into the financial aspects of the companies, leaving that discussion to others.

data.title: U.S. Senators Urge Review of UAE's Acquisition of Trump Family Crypto Firm Stake
data.description:
U.S. Senators Elizabeth Warren and Andy Kim have called for an investigation into a transaction involving the acquisition of a stake in a Trump family crypto enterprise by UAE entities. According to PANews, the senators addressed a letter to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, urging scrutiny of the deal for potential national security risks.

The transaction reportedly involves a UAE company purchasing a 49% stake in World Liberty Financial for $500 million. Warren and Kim have requested Bessent, who oversees the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS), to determine if the acquisition warrants a CFIUS review. They emphasized the need for a comprehensive, thorough, and impartial investigation if deemed necessary.

The senators have asked Bessent to respond by March 5, addressing several questions, including whether CFIUS has reviewed the transaction or provided any recommendations to U.S. President Donald Trump. They expressed concerns about the potential national security implications, particularly regarding the access to sensitive personal information collected by World Liberty Financial.

data.title: Hermès Expands Product Line Beyond Bags and Clothing
data.description: Crypto KOL Danny (@agintender) posted on X about Hermès, expressing surprise at the luxury brand's diverse offerings beyond its well-known bags and clothing. The post highlights Hermès' expansion into various product categories, showcasing the brand's versatility and broadening consumer perception. Hermès, traditionally recognized for its high-end fashion items, continues to innovate and diversify its product range, appealing to a wider audience.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Transactions Linked to Human Trafficking Surge by 85% in 2025
data.description: Chainalysis has revealed a significant increase in cryptocurrency transactions associated with services suspected of human trafficking, rising by 85% in 2025. According to NS3.AI, these transactions have reached hundreds of millions of dollars, with the majority of illicit activities concentrated in Southeast Asia. The report underscores the growing use of cryptocurrencies by illicit actors, despite ongoing regulatory efforts to curb such activities. Telegram-based international escort services are often used to facilitate these transactions, highlighting the challenges faced by authorities in combating human trafficking in the digital age.

data.title: SkyAI and Mask Network See Significant Unfollows on X
data.description:
In the past week, SkyAI, an AI blockchain data infrastructure, has experienced the highest number of unfollows from top influencers on X, according to ChainCatcher. Influential figures in the crypto space, such as KOL WolfyXBT, KOL Enheng456, and Crypto Pumpman, are among those who have unfollowed the project.

Additionally, Mask Network has also seen a significant number of unfollows from top personalities on the platform.

data.title: STOCKS | Bridgewater Increases Holdings in AI Stocks and Gold Producers
data.description:
Bridgewater Associates has disclosed its latest U.S. stock holdings report, revealing significant changes in its investment strategy during the fourth quarter ending December 31, 2022. According to Jin10, the firm increased its stakes in popular AI stocks such as Nvidia (NVDA.O), Amazon (AMZN.O), and Micron Technology (MU.O). Additionally, Bridgewater capitalized on rising gold prices by boosting its investment in Newmont Corporation, one of the world's largest gold producers.

The report indicates that Bridgewater's total portfolio value rose to approximately $27.4 billion, up from $25.5 billion in the previous quarter. During the third quarter, the firm added 191 new positions and increased its holdings in 450 stocks. Conversely, Bridgewater reduced its stakes in 395 stocks and completely exited 165 positions.

data.title: Solana Spot ETF Records Significant Daily Net Inflow
data.description:
Solana spot ETFs have experienced notable financial movements, with a daily net inflow of $1.5716 million, according to ChainCatcher. Data from SoSoValue reveals that the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) saw a daily net inflow of $1.6873 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $683 million. Conversely, the VanEck Solana ETF (VSOL) recorded a daily net outflow of $554,100, with its historical total net inflow reaching $20.2125 million.

As of the latest report, the total net asset value of Solana spot ETFs stands at $721 million, with a net asset ratio of 1.5%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $875 million.

data.title: Ethereum Whale Profits from Strategic Trading Moves
data.description: A cryptocurrency whale recently executed a strategic trading maneuver in the Ethereum market. According to NS3.AI, the investor purchased 5,089.56 ETH when prices were near a low of $1,953.65. Subsequently, the whale sold 4,000 ETH at a short-term peak of $2,058.91, securing a profit of $421,000. The remaining 1,089.56 ETH position currently holds an unrealized floating profit of $104,000. This activity underscores the potential for significant gains through opportunistic trading within short time frames in the ETH market.

data.title: The US Solomon Islands (SOL) spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $1.5716 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (February 13th, Eastern Time), the total net inflow into SOL spot ETFs was $1.5716 million. Among them, the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) saw a net inflow of $1.6873 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $683 million. The VanEck Solana ETF (VSOL) experienced a net outflow of $554,100, with a historical total net inflow of $20.2125 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of SOL spot ETFs is $721 million, with an SOL net asset value ratio of 1.50%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $875 million.

data.title: Product Manager X: We plan to further combat spam and will conduct testing; we recommend temporarily suspending the integration of chatbot programs.
data.description: Nikita Bier, X Product Lead and Solana Consultant, announced that X will introduce more detection mechanisms for automated behavior and spam, and will continue to strengthen related measures. If the system detects that an account has not been interacted with manually, the account and its associated accounts may be suspended, even if it is only used for technical testing or experimentation. The X platform aims to support legitimate proxy and automation use cases in the long term, but related capabilities still need time to be improved. In the meantime, developers are advised to postpone integrating bot programs. If business needs dictate otherwise, the official API interface should be used first.

data.title: The US Senate and House of Representatives adjourn starting today, making a shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security almost a certainty.
data.description: Because the Democratic and Republican parties in the United States could not reach a consensus on relevant reform plans, the Senate failed to pass an appropriations bill for the Department of Homeland Security on the 12th, local time, with only one day left before its funding expired. Given that both the House and Senate will begin a 10-day recess on the 14th, media outlets widely believe that this makes a shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security virtually inevitable. It is understood that the Department of Homeland Security may be shut down from 00:00 local time on the 14th, which is 13:00 Beijing time on the 14th. (Jinshi)

data.title: First Gen Acquires 40% Stake in Prime Infrastructure's Hydropower Portfolio for $1.3 Billion
data.description: First Gen Corporation has announced its acquisition of a 40% stake in Prime Infrastructure Capital's hydropower portfolio, valued at $1.3 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this transaction for the Philippine companies involved. This strategic move is expected to enhance First Gen's renewable energy capabilities and expand its presence in the hydropower sector. The deal underscores the growing interest in sustainable energy solutions within the region.

data.title: BihuoPro, a project focused on encrypted data infrastructure, has secured $5 million in strategic investment from Gemhead Capital.
data.description: Gemhead Capital has officially announced a $5 million strategic investment in BihuoPro. BihuoPro is developing next-generation private domain financial data and sentiment intelligence infrastructure for the altcoin market. By structuring community behavior into tradable signals, BihuoPro helps institutions and advanced traders understand consensus formation within private communities, the cross-influence between people, events, and assets, and early sentiment shifts before price movements. Positioning itself as the "Bloomberg of market sentiment," BihuoPro is dedicated to digitizing the invisible consensus layer of the cryptocurrency space. Gemhead Capital will support BihuoPro's next phase of growth.

data.title: BTC Options Market Structure to Impact Spot Liquidity
data.description:
Crypto KOL Murphy posted on X that the BTC options market's net premium heat map indicates a shift in investor sentiment, which could affect spot liquidity. As of February 12, the net premium for the $62,000-$66,000 range has turned positive, now at $8.72 million, compared to a previous negative $48 million. Conversely, the $58,000-$62,000 range has shifted from positive to negative, currently at -$20.46 million from a previous $17.37 million.

This change suggests that market makers have moved to a short gamma structure in the $62,000-$65,000 range, while a new long gamma structure has emerged in the $58,000-$62,000 range. In a long gamma structure, market makers buy spot to maintain delta neutrality when prices fall, providing price support. Conversely, a short gamma structure can amplify volatility.

Observations from December 2025, when a similar long gamma structure was present, showed BTC fluctuating between $82,000 and $87,000. Currently, the net premiums for the $69,000-$73,000 range (-$2,972) and the $58,000-$62,000 range (-$20.46 million) are smaller compared to December 2025, indicating the formation of an initial volatility compression structure.

If this trend continues, the market may see new dynamics between the $58,000-$62,000 and $69,000-$73,000 ranges in the near future, with stronger resistance expected between $82,000 and $87,000 if a breakout occurs.
